zh.Hinton Woodford, Northamptonshire, Engla: 英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾伍德福特
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John de Hynton, 3rd of Huntingdon於大約1270年出生在英國亨廷格多史爾時，他的父親Sir John de Hynton The Younger, of Hynton-Woodford22歲，母親Petronilla de Massingham21歲。他於大約1250年與Margaret de Coteford結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。他於1340年登記服兵役。他於1338年在比利時安特維爾被列為knighted by king edward iii。他去世於1350年享年81歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John de Hynton, 3rd of Huntingdon于大约1270年出生在英国亨廷格多史爾时，他的父亲Sir John de Hynton The Younger, of Hynton-Woodford22岁，母亲Petronilla de Massingham21岁。他于大约1250年与Margaret de Coteford结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。他于1340年登记服兵役。他于1338年在比利时安特維爾被列为knighted by king edward iii。他去世于1350年享年81岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Hynton,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the many places called Hinton (for example, in Shropshire, Herefordshire, Gloucestershire, Somerset, Hampshire, and Northamptonshire). Some of the placenames, such as those in Northamptonshire, Shropshire, and Herefordshire, derive from Old English hīna, genitive plural form of hīwan ‘household, religious community’, + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’ (compare Hine as the first element). Others, such as those in Gloucestershire, Somerset, and Hampshire, derive from Old English hēan, dative form of hēah ‘high’ + tūn.

variants.name: Hine
meanings: English (Devon): occupational name from Middle English, Old English hīne ‘servant, member of a household’, also ‘farm laborer’ (such as a herdsman or shepherd). Originally a collective term for a body of servants, from an Old English plural noun, hīwan ‘household’, in early modern English it commonly acquired excrescent -d (see Hinde ). Evidence from Nottinghamshire shows that some bearers of the name were wealthy freemen, which suggests that their eponymous ancestors were not farmhands but senior members of a lord's household. From the late 15th century onward in western England, from south to north, a hine or hind also denoted a farm steward or manager, a sense that may be relevant to the surname in northwestern England, where hereditary naming had not been fully established by the 16th century.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John de Hynton, 3rd of Huntingdon was born about 1270, in Huntingdonshire, England, his father, Sir John de Hynton The Younger, of Hynton-Woodford, was 22 and his mother, Petronilla de Massingham, was 21. He married Margaret de Coteford about 1250. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. He registered for military service in 1340. In 1338, at the age of 69, his occupation is listed as knighted by king edward iii in Antwerp, Belgium. He died in 1350, at the age of 81.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John de Hynton, 3rd of Huntingdon est né environ 1270, à Huntingdonshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir John de Hynton The Younger, of Hynton-Woodford, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Petronilla de Massingham, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Margaret de Coteford environ 1250. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1340. En 1338, à l’âge de 69 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est knighted by king edward iii à Anvers, Belgique. Il est décédé en 1350, à l’âge de 81 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John de Hynton, 3rd of Huntingdon um 1270 in Huntingdonshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir John de Hynton The Younger, of Hynton-Woodford 22 und seine Mutter Petronilla de Massingham 21. Er hat um 1250 Margaret de Coteford geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Er hat sich 1340 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. 1338, als er 69 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als knighted by king edward iii in Antwerpen, Belgien aufgeführt. Er starb 1350 im Alter von 81 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John de Hynton, 3rd of Huntingdon è nato il circa 1270 nel Huntingdonshire, England, il suo padre, Sir John de Hynton The Younger, of Hynton-Woodford, aveva 22 anni e il suo madre, Petronilla de Massingham, aveva 21 anni. Lui sposato Margaret de Coteford circa 1250. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1340. Nel 1338, all'età di 69 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come knighted by king edward iii nel Anversa, Belgio. Morì nel 1350, all'età di 81 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John de Hynton, 3rd of Huntingdonが約1270年にイギリスハンティングドンシュアイアで生まれた時，父Sir John de Hynton The Younger, of Hynton-Woodfordは22歳，母Petronilla de Massinghamは21歳であった。約1250年にMargaret de Cotefordと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼は1340年に兵役登録をした。1338年，69歳の時，ベルギーアントウエルペンでの職業はknighted by king edward iiiと記録されている。彼は1350年に81歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John de Hynton, 3rd of Huntingdon(이)가 대략 1270년 영국 헌틴그도실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John de Hynton The Younger, of Hynton-Woodford(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Petronilla de Massingham(은)는 21세였다. 그는 대략 1250년 Margaret de Coteford(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1340년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 1338년, 69세의 나이로 그의 직업은 벨기에 앤트워펜에서 knighted by king edward iii이었다. 그는 1350년 81세의 나이로 사망했다.

mn.Hinton Woodford, Northamptonshire, Engla: Woodford, North Hamtunscire, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John de Hynton, 3rd of Huntingdon nasceu aproximadamente 1270, em Huntingdonshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John de Hynton The Younger, of Hynton-Woodford, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Petronilla de Massingham, tinha 21 anos. Ele casou-se com Margaret de Coteford aproximadamente 1250. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1340. Em 1338, com 69 anos, sua profissão aparece como knighted by king edward iii em Antuérpia, Bélgica. Ele faleceu em 1350, com 81 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John de Hynton, 3rd of Huntingdon родился около 1270 г. в местности хантингдоншир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir John de Hynton The Younger, of Hynton-Woodford было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Petronilla de Massingham, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret de Coteford около 1250 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. В 1340 году он поступил на военную службу. В 1338 году, в возрасте 69 лет, работал по профессии knighted by king edward iii в местности антвирпен, Бельгия. Он умер в 1350 г. в возрасте 81 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir John de Hynton, 3rd of Huntingdon nació aproximadamente 1270, en Huntingdonshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John de Hynton The Younger, of Hynton-Woodford, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Petronilla de Massingham, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Margaret de Coteford aproximadamente 1250. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1340. En 1338, a la edad de 69 años, su ocupación registrada era knighted by king edward iii en Amberes, Bélgica. Murió en 1350, a la edad de 81 años.
